description: APOL1 kidney-risk panel (African-ancestry-gated, recessive): G1 = rs73885319 (S342G, risk G) + rs60910145 (I384M, risk G, in cis); G2 = rs71785313 (6-bp deletion p.Asn388_Tyr389del; off-chip on most arrays; gnomAD also labels this deletion rs1317778148); N264K modifier = rs73885316 (risk-attenuating allele A/Lys). High risk requires TWO risk alleles across G1/G2 (recessive). G1 dosage is counted from the rs73885319 tag SNP ONLY in total_risk_dosage; rs60910145 is read for transparency/QC but deliberately NOT summed, because the two G1 SNPs are in cis on one haplotype — summing both would double-count G1 and mis-call a single-G1 heterozygote (one risk allele = low risk) as two-allele high-risk. N264K present attenuates G2-associated risk. Common risk alleles, not ClinVar P/LP -> clinvar_significance=NULL. EXPANSION_STRATEGY.md §9 / roadmap #27.

ancestry_gate.note: APOL1 G1/G2 risk alleles arose on, and are validated in, recent African-ancestry backgrounds and are near-absent elsewhere; this high-risk genotype is reported as actionable only for inferred African ancestry.

genotype_models.odds_ratio: Focal segmental glomerulosclerosis OR 10.5 (95% CI 6.0–18.4); hypertension-attributed end-stage kidney disease OR 7.3 (95% CI 5.6–9.5), versus the low-risk (0–1 risk allele) genotype
genotype_models.absolute_risk_context: These are relative odds for specific kidney diseases, not an absolute or lifetime risk. Even with two risk alleles, most people never develop kidney disease — penetrance is incomplete and usually requires a 'second hit' (an inflammatory/interferon state, certain infections). This is a risk genotype, not a diagnosis.

modifier.unassessed_caveat: The N264K modifier (rs73885316), which strongly attenuates G2-associated kidney risk, was not assessed on this array, so this high-risk estimate may be overstated.

reclassify.risk_classification: APOL1 risk attenuated by N264K modifier
reclassify.finding_text: You carry an APOL1 two-risk-allele genotype, but you also carry the N264K modifier (rs73885316), which strongly attenuates G2-associated kidney-disease risk — this reclassifies the genotype toward low risk (Nat Commun 2023). Discuss interpretation with a clinician or genetic counselor.

partial_disclosure.finding_text: You carry one APOL1 risk allele ({genotype}), but at least one other APOL1 variant was not typed on this array — in particular the G2 6-bp deletion (rs71785313), which is frequently off-chip. Because APOL1 kidney risk is recessive (two risk alleles are needed), your status cannot be determined from these data: it would be high-risk if you also carry an untyped G2 (or second G1) risk allele, and low-risk if you do not. This is not a low-risk result. Consider sequencing-based confirmation and discuss with a clinician or genetic counselor.
